__fallback__.headline: Offline maize guidance
__fallback__.next_step: Retake a clear maize leaf photo and confirm in the field before applying chemical treatment.
__fallback__.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
actions: Retake a close maize leaf photo in even light if symptoms are unclear.
Mark affected rows and avoid unnecessary movement through wet foliage.
Avoid broad spraying based only on the offline result.
monitoring: Check the same rows again within 24 to 48 hours.
Inspect neighboring maize plants for similar lesions.
prevention: Clean tools before moving between blocks.
Reduce prolonged leaf wetness where possible.
escalate_if: Symptoms spread quickly across multiple rows.
Defoliation or severe canopy damage increases rapidly.
notes: This is crop-specific offline guidance and still needs field confirmation.
__fallback__.verification_note: Draft treatment guidance. Expert agronomy review is required before pesticide use.

Corn_(maize)___healthy.headline: Leaf appears healthy
Corn_(maize)___healthy.next_step: No disease class was flagged. Continue routine scouting and rescan only if symptoms appear.
actions: Rescan only if new spots, streaks, or yellowing begin to spread.
monitoring: Inspect upper and middle leaves during the next field round.
Watch after humid or rainy weather for fresh lesions.
prevention: Keep tools clean and remove severely damaged leaves only where practical.
Maintain balanced fertility to reduce crop stress.
escalate_if: New lesions spread across several plants.
Nearby plants begin to show the same pattern.
notes: Healthy predictions can still miss early field symptoms. Keep scouting.
Corn_(maize)___healthy.verification_note: No pesticide treatment is recommended for a healthy result.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.headline: Likely gray leaf spot
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.next_step: Verify soon and track whether narrow rectangular lesions are spreading up the canopy.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.active_ingredient: Foliar fungicide such as propiconazole, azoxystrobin, pyraclostrobin, or a locally registered premix for gray leaf spot.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.dosage: Use the locally registered label rate for the selected maize foliar fungicide.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.pre_harvest_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label PHI for maize.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.re_entry_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label REI.
actions: Mark affected rows so spread can be checked again quickly.
Keep records of which growth stage is being affected.
Avoid rushing into chemical treatment before field confirmation.
monitoring: Check whether lesions are moving from lower to upper leaves.
Watch whether humid weather is followed by rapid lesion expansion.
prevention: Reduce carryover of infected residue where possible.
Keep field movement clean to avoid spreading spores between blocks.
escalate_if: Lesions begin covering large leaf areas across several rows.
Upper canopy damage increases close to tasseling or grain fill.
notes: Gray leaf spot often becomes more damaging when canopy humidity stays high.
Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.verification_note: Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.headline: Likely common rust
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.next_step: Verify in the field and track whether rust pustules are increasing on younger leaves.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.active_ingredient: Foliar fungicide such as propiconazole, tebuconazole, azoxystrobin, or a locally registered premix for common rust.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.dosage: Use the locally registered label rate for the selected maize rust fungicide.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.pre_harvest_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label PHI for maize.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.re_entry_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label REI.
actions: Inspect both sides of affected leaves for raised rust pustules.
Mark affected rows so disease spread can be checked again.
Avoid unnecessary treatment until symptoms and severity are confirmed.
monitoring: Watch younger leaves for increasing pustule numbers.
prevention: Reduce field-to-field spread through tool hygiene where practical.
Keep crop nutrition balanced to avoid extra stress.
escalate_if: Rust pustules increase quickly across many plants.
notes: Severity matters more than single-leaf presence. Confirm field pressure before treatment.
Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.verification_note: Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.headline: Possible northern leaf blight
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.next_step: Treat as urgent. Verify quickly and monitor whether large cigar-shaped lesions are increasing.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.active_ingredient: Foliar fungicide such as propiconazole, azoxystrobin, pyraclostrobin, or a locally registered premix for northern leaf blight.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.dosage: Use the locally registered label rate for the selected maize foliar fungicide.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.pre_harvest_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label PHI for maize.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.re_entry_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label REI.
actions: Mark affected rows and prioritize same-day field review.
Track whether lesions are moving into the upper canopy.
Avoid delay if severe spread is occurring during favorable weather.
monitoring: Inspect daily when weather is cool and humid.
Watch lesion growth on upper leaves near tasseling or grain fill.
prevention: Keep tools and movement between blocks as clean as possible.
escalate_if: Upper canopy lesions increase rapidly across several rows.
Defoliation risk rises during critical yield stages.
notes: Northern leaf blight can become high impact when upper leaves are affected.
Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.verification_note: Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
